name: Mark's Famous Po-Boy
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the beef into thin strips, allowing it to be slightly frozen for best results.
2. Place sliced roast in a bowl with Worcestershire sauce and marinate for 15 to 20 minutes.
3. In a skillet, sauté onions, bell peppers, and half a teaspoon of seasoning salt in peanut oil until cooked through. Remove vegetables from the skillet.
4. Add the remaining seasoning salt to the meat and cook until just browned.
5. Return the vegetables to the skillet with the meat and cook for another 10 minutes.
6. Place French bread open-faced under the broiler until crispy.
7. Spread the meat mixture onto the bread, top with Swiss cheese slices, and broil until the cheese has melted.
8. Add a layer of mayonnaise and serve with leftover juices.
